quality_note: First promoted equation canon. Records are source-located candidates unless explicitly marked reviewed. Exact typography and page anchors still require scan verification.

records.title: Velocity, Frequency, and Wave Length
records.original_form: f = S / lambda; equivalently S = f lambda

source_ref.location: Lecture I, cleaned OCR lines 248-259 and 697-703

records.why_canonical: This relation ties electric waves, visible light, ultraviolet, X-rays, and low-frequency AC fields into one radiation scale.

records.title: Rectangular Complex Quantity
records.source_title: Theory and Calculation of Alternating Current Phenomena

source_ref.location: Chapter V, Symbolic Method, cleaned OCR lines 285-290

records.why_canonical: This is the bridge from sine-wave geometry to algebraic AC calculation.

records.title: The Operator j
records.source_title: Theory and Calculation of Alternating Current Phenomena
records.modern_form: j = sqrt(-1), used as a 90 degree rotation operator in phasor analysis

source_ref.location: Chapter V, Symbolic Method, cleaned OCR lines 317-333 and promoted Fig. 24 crop

records.why_canonical: Steinmetz makes the imaginary unit a physical phase operator before it becomes mere algebra.

records.title: Inductive Reactance
records.source_title: Theory and Calculation of Alternating Current Phenomena
records.modern_form: X_L = omega L = 2 pi f L
records.status: source-located candidate; OCR symbol defects present

source_ref.location: Chapter V, Symbolic Method, cleaned OCR lines 292-315 and 373-376

records.why_canonical: It fixes reactance as frequency-dependent field opposition rather than resistance.

records.title: Condensive Reactance
records.source_title: Theory and Calculation of Alternating Current Phenomena
records.status: source-located candidate; OCR symbol defects present

source_ref.location: Chapter V, Symbolic Method, cleaned OCR lines 351-380

records.why_canonical: It preserves Steinmetz's older term for capacitive reactance and its opposite sign from inductive reactance.

records.title: Complex Impedance
records.source_title: Theory and Calculation of Alternating Current Phenomena

source_ref.location: Chapter V, Symbolic Method, cleaned OCR lines 317-333

records.why_canonical: This is the compact formula behind modern AC circuit analysis.

records.title: Symbolic Ohm's Law
records.source_title: Theory and Calculation of Alternating Current Phenomena
records.original_form: E = ZI, I = E / Z, Z = E / I

source_ref.location: Chapter V, Symbolic Method, cleaned OCR lines 326-383

records.why_canonical: It restores Ohm's law for alternating quantities by carrying phase inside the symbols.

records.title: Admittance As Reciprocal Impedance
records.source_title: Theory and Calculation of Alternating Current Phenomena
records.modern_form: Y = 1 / Z; sign convention depends on reactance convention

source_ref.location: Chapter VIII, Admittance, Conductance, Susceptance, cleaned OCR lines 48-222

records.why_canonical: It prevents the common error of treating conductance and susceptance as shallow reciprocals of resistance and reactance.

records.title: Conductance and Susceptance From Impedance
records.source_title: Theory and Calculation of Alternating Current Phenomena
records.modern_form: For Z = R + jX and Y = G - jB: G = R/(R^2+X^2), B = X/(R^2+X^2)

source_ref.location: Chapter VIII relation plus algebraic reciprocal; existing public derivation

records.why_canonical: It explains why admittance is structurally different from resistance alone.

records.title: AC Power Equation
records.source_title: Theory and Calculation of Alternating Current Phenomena

source_ref.location: Chapter I, Introduction, cleaned OCR lines 248-254

records.why_canonical: It makes power factor a physical and mathematical term, not a billing afterthought.

records.title: Effective Resistance From Real Power
records.source_title: Theory and Calculation of Alternating Current Phenomena

source_ref.location: Chapter I, Introduction, cleaned OCR lines 236-246 and Chapter XII lines 64-85

records.why_canonical: It captures Steinmetz's distinction between true ohmic resistance and total real-power loss.

records.title: Capacity Susceptance
records.source_title: Theory and Calculation of Alternating Current Phenomena
records.modern_form: B_C = omega C = 2 pi f C

source_ref.location: Chapter XIV, Dielectric Losses, cleaned OCR lines 209-221 and 595-599

records.why_canonical: It links electrostatic capacity to admittance language and dielectric loss analysis.
